new_rules.rule: Boxed review and repair bundles must fail closed unless every referenced evidence file and required authority file is present in the manifest.
new_rules.applies_to: Bundle builders, boxed manifest generators, review/repair evidence collection, authority-file inclusion checks
new_rules.do: When building a review or repair box, gather every path cited by the diff summary, compliance map, fix-items map, and requested authority list, compare that set to the boxed manifest, and stop the run if any referenced file is missing.
new_rules.dont: Build a bundle that omits a file named in the compliance map or requested authority list and continue as if the reviewer can still verify the claim from inside the box.
new_rules.source: Round 5: boxed review and repair bundles could omit referenced evidence without blocking the workflow

new_rules.rule: Terminal artifacts, postmortems, and KPI summaries must be derived from one canonical result/evidence model on every exit path, including blocked and pre-round exits.
new_rules.applies_to: Top-level orchestrators, terminal result builders, postmortem writers, KPI/count aggregation, machine-facing run artifacts
new_rules.do: Create one final run-result object that contains the full issue arrays and terminal state, then compute postmortem content and counters directly from that object for success, blocked, and zero-round exits.
new_rules.dont: Maintain separate ad hoc counters and artifact writers so a blocked run skips its postmortem or reports counts that do not match the actual issue arrays.
new_rules.source: Round 5: non-happy-path artifacts and KPI counts could disagree with the actual run state

existing_rules_covering.explanation: Verdict tokens are contract-defined workflow vocabulary. Accepting an off-contract alias instead of rejecting it breaks the single-sourced status set and violates the requirement that unhandled values fail closed.

The audit contract requires canonical audit provenance fields end-to-end. Passing `reviewMode: null` without an explicit contract-sanctioned representation loses required audit data instead of preserving it faithfully.

Duplicating `uniqueStrings` in multiple live modules with different semantics is the exact mirrored-logic divergence this rule is meant to prevent; shared governed behavior should come from one canonical implementation or be explicitly separated.

no_rule_needed.reason: This is a local documentation-drift issue. The fix is to align or remove the stale comment, but it is too specific and low-impact to justify a new governance rule.
